2605.25829.title: OASIS: Observation-Action Space Alignment via SE(3) Trajectory Prediction for Robotic Manipulation
2605.25829.paper_summary: Recent vision-language-action (VLA) models and world action models (WAMs) advance robotic manipulation by enriching intermediate representations with auxiliary spatial features or future visual-state prediction. However, these representations largely remain within the observation space and do not share the rigid-body geometry of the action space, forcing the action decoder to implicitly recover this geometry. We propose OASIS, a visuomotor policy that aligns the intermediate representation with the action space via $SE(3)$ end-effector trajectory prediction. OASIS couples a 3D-aware feature encoder that fuses vision-language and metric-depth features with an $SE(3)$ trajectory predictor that produces a camera-frame end-effector trajectory. Conditioned on the predictor's pose-supervised hidden states, the action decoder generates action chunks consistent with rigid-body motion. Across simulation and real-world experiments, OASIS outperforms VLA and WAM baselines in success rate and out-of-distribution generalization. Our project page is available at https://npuhandsome.github.io/OASIS_web.
2605.25829.paper_summary_zh: 最近的視覺-語言-行動（VLA）模型和世界行動模型（WAMs）通過豐富中間表示，加入輔助空間特徵或未來視覺狀態預測，推進了機器人操作。然而，這些表示在很大程度上仍然停留在觀察空間中，並未共享行動空間的剛體幾何，迫使行動解碼器隱式地恢復這一幾何。我們提出了OASIS，一種視覺運動策略，通過$SE(3)$末端執行器軌跡預測將中間表示與行動空間對齊。OASIS結合了一個3D感知特徵編碼器，該編碼器融合了視覺-語言和度量深度特徵，以及一個$SE(3)$軌跡預測器，該預測器生成相機框架的末端執行器軌跡。在預測器的姿態監督隱藏狀態的條件下，行動解碼器生成與剛體運動一致的行動片段。在模擬和現實世界的實驗中，OASIS在成功率和超出分佈的泛化方面超越了VLA和WAM基準。我們的項目頁面可在https://npuhandsome.github.io/OASIS_web找到。
2605.25829.author: Xinzhe Chen et.al.
2605.25829.authors: Xinzhe Chen, Sihua Ren, Liqi Huang, Haowen Sun, Mingyang Li, Xingyu Chen, Zeyang Liu, Xuguang Lan
